title: What Barry can help with — Barry Gardiner
text_excerpt: What Barry can help with — Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Surgery Information: Barry hosts regular surgeries at Brent Civic Centre, Engineers Way, Wembley HA9 0FJ . To request a surgery appointment please contact Barry with details of your case and any copies of relevant correspondence to either barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk or via post to Barry Gardiner MP House of Commons SW1A 0AA Help & Advice: Who can Barry help? Barry can only help in cases raised by people who live in his constituency, which covers the wards of Alperton, Barnhill, Fryent, Kenton, Northwick Park, Preston, Queensbury, Sudbury, and Wembley Central. If you are not a constituent please contact your local MP. You can find out who your local MP is by entering your postcode here . Please note that if you are contacting Barry on behalf of a friend or family member he will need their signed and written consent before he is able to take action. Barry can help you with any matter for which Parliament or central government is responsible, such as matters relating to : - Benefits, pensions or allowances – please have your National Insurance Number ready when contacting the office. - Immigration and Asylum - please have your Full Name/Date of birth/Country of Origin/Home Office reference number ready when contacting the office. - Queries and complaints about the NHS - Issues regarding tax - Education Barry doesn't have any control over Brent Council and if your problem relates to the local authority, the first contact should be with your local councillors. If you are not satisfied with their response Barry may be able to assist. Please provide proof that you've approached the council before contacting our office. You can check who your ward councillors are here Brent Council has responsibility for matters such as: Housing – allocations, repairs, problems with landlords Finance – Council tax, Housing and Council Tax Benefit, rent arrears Schools – allocation of school places, complaints Social services – carers, Child Protection Environmental health - noise nuisance, trading standards, public health Planning Waste Services - refuse collection/ street cleaning/fly-tipping Public highways – potholes, road repairs, street lighting, traffic problems Leisure facilities – libraries, parks, sports centres How can Barry help? Barry and his team can help constituents by raising concerns with the relevant department or official or writing to the relevant Minister, many problems can be solved in this way. Our constituent cases are dealt with in terms of how urgent the case is, so please note that due to the volume of correspondence Barry receives, helping constituents with urgent personal problems has to take priority over responding to correspondence on political issues. Some Useful Numbers Emergencies Police, fire, first aid emergencies 999 To report a crime (not in an emergency) 101 Gas Emergencies 080 0111 9999 NHS 111 111 Health Care Northwick Park Hospital 020 8864 3232 Brent Clinical Commissioning Group 020 8900 5300 Transport TfL (Tube and buses) 034 3222 1234 National Rail Enquiries 034 5748 4950 Utility providers UK Power Networks (emergencies from mobiles) 080 0316 3105 UK Power Networks (Customer Services) 080 0028 4587 Thames Water (Blocked Drains) 080 0316 9800 Affinity Water (emergencies) 0345 357 2407 Advice National Debtline 080 8808 4000 24 Hour National Domestic Violence Helpline 080 8200 0247 Asian Women’s Resource Centre 020 8961 5701 Solace Women’s Aid 0
